mnemonics.title: IECT Phases
mnemonics.content: Inception → Elaboration → Construction → Transition. Remember: 'I E C T' spells 'I Eat Cookies Today' - Each phase produces an 'edible' incremental deliverable.

mnemonics.title: 9 Core Workflows Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: BREAD: Business modeling, Requirements, Environment, Analysis & Design. Add: Implementation, Test, Deployment, Project Management, Configuration Management. Remember: 'BRAAE ITCP' - 'BRought AA Elephant In The Circus Parade'

mnemonics.title: UP Principles
mnemonics.content: U-P-U: Use-case driven, Process is iterative & incremental, Architecture-centric, Risk-driven. Remember: 'UP Students Learn A Lot' (Use-case, Process, risk, Architecture, iterative/incremental)

acronyms.usage: A generic iterative and incremental software development process framework
IBM's commercial implementation of UP with additional tooling and process guidance
Key artifact created in Elaboration phase describing system architecture
Standard modeling language used in UP for visual representation of artifacts

visualTips.visualization: Picture a staircase: Inception (step 1 - establish foundation), Elaboration (step 2 - build framework), Construction (step 3 - add rooms/furnish), Transition (step 4 - move in). Each step builds on the previous.
Think of a spiral: each loop (iteration) covers all activities from center outward, growing larger with each pass. Each loop produces an executable increment closer to final product.
A wheel with use cases as the hub; all spokes (design, implementation, test, deployment) radiate from and connect back to the use-case hub. If a spoke breaks, the wheel doesn't work.

analogies.analogy: Building a house: Waterfall is building the entire blueprint, then foundation, then walls, then roof—complete before moving to next. UP is like building a small model first (foundation + basic frame), then adding rooms incrementally, refining each time based on what works.
Like constructing a building's skeleton (beams, columns) before adding walls, plumbing, electrical. The skeleton defines what can be added and where—without it, rooms collapse.

Like a ship's navigation: identify icebergs (risks) early on the voyage, navigate around them or prepare solutions before reaching them, rather than hitting them and dealing with the damage mid-voyage.
